steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The underlined portion is: ‘To conclude that this approach accounts for the ethereal qualities now synonymous with the Highwaymen aesthetic is tempting but inaccurate, as Hair's methods weren't universally practiced by his affiliates.’ The question asks what function this sentence plays in the passage as a whole.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — It details evidence that contradicts a claim mentioned earlier in the text about a long-standing disagreement within a group of artists.: No disagreement among artists is mentioned anywhere in the passage. This is invented. ✗
  • Choice B — It considers and rebuts an interpretation of the effect of a painting technique mentioned earlier in the text on the perception of work by a group of artists.: The underlined sentence acknowledges a tempting interpretation (fast painting creates the ethereal aesthetic), calls it inaccurate, and provides the counterevidence (Buckner achieved the same look without the technique). That is exactly considering and rebutting an interpretation of a technique's effect. ✓
  • Choice C — It establishes a contrast between the aesthetic qualities of works by artists who were central to a movement introduced earlier in the text and those of an artist who was more peripheral to that movement.: The passage does not frame Buckner as peripheral; and the contrast drawn is about technique, not about how central or marginal each artist was to the movement. ✗
  • Choice D — It explains how an artist mentioned earlier in the text developed a distinctive style by adapting a particular approach to painting originated by his colleagues.: The passage says Hair originated fast painting and others adopted it — not that Hair adapted something from colleagues. This reverses the direction. ✗
That matches Choice B — It considers and rebuts an interpretation of the effect of a painting technique mentioned earlier in the text on the perception of work by a group of artists..
